title: hieracium substellatum subsp. sublacteum
intro: Hieracium substellatum subsp. sublacteum, also known as a lesser Hawkweed or Hieracium sublacteum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Scotland and Ireland. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Hieracium substellatum subsp. sublacteum is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in salves and ointments for topical application.
